ChangeRequest.name: Subtype primary key - special treatment for `Short position accounting classification`
ChangeRequest.comment:
<!-- See: documentation on [Issues in GitLab](https://gitlab.sofa.dev/sdd-bird-team/bird-logical-data-model/-/wikis/Issues-in-GitLab) -->
# Overview
It seems that the primary key of the entity type `Short position accounting classification` (which is a subtype of the entity type `Accounting classification for financial liabilities`) is subject to a different treatment than the primary key of other subtypes.
### Contact person
dominik.lin-ully@oenb.at
### Background information
The primary key attribute `Short position accounting classification`.`Short position accounting classification` is created as a primary key attribute, although the entity type `Short position accounting classification` is a subtype of the entity type `Accounting classification for financial liabilities` which is a non-abstract entity type and therefore already comprises a primary key, namely `Accounting classification`.
The situation is illustrated in the following picture:
![Accounting_classification_reference_entity_hierarchy](/uploads/9d814d3c78162a78157315a8f5cbd261/Accounting_classification_reference_entity_hierarchy.png)
**Figure 1:** `Accounting classification` entity type hierarchy
This treatment seems to be incompatible / overlapping with using constraints when only a subset of a reference data entity type's allowed values are needed - e.g., `European Central Bank`.`International organisation code` limited to the value `ECB (European Central Bank) (4F)` via constraint.
Furthermore, this treatment may result in inconsistencies. For example, the data types of the two primary keys are different, i.e., `Short position accounting classification` is assigned to 'String (2)' while `Accounting classification` is assigned to 'String (3)'.
### Points/Questions to resolve
* assess if this treatment / construct is used for the same use case as using constraints when only a subset of a reference data entity type's allowed values is required * if yes, decide on one approach and implement it consistently * if no, no action is required and the treatment / construct has a defined purpose. Please add a description of the situations where this treatment / construct should be applied, e.g., in the BIRD LDM design principles document to make sure it is properly documented. * in any case, please fix the inconsistencies related to the assigned data types of the primary keys
### Related issues
### Before closing checklist - [ ] Assignee for review - [ ] Assignee to close - [ ] Other actions
